summary_en: ¥250 billion upper limit; 45 million shares (2.26% of S/O); execution window 21 May 2026 – 27 April 2027; open-market method (ToSTNeT-3 / market purchases); decision made 20 May 2026 by board.
title_en: Notice Regarding Open-Market Purchase of Own Shares
translation_en_brief:
On 20 May 2026, Shin-Etsu Chemical's board resolved to conduct an open-market purchase of up to 45 million of its own shares (approximately 2.26% of outstanding shares) for a maximum aggregate consideration of ¥250 billion, to be executed between 21 May 2026 and 27 April 2027 using open-market methods (ToSTNeT-3 and market purchases on the Tokyo Stock Exchange).

The company noted that this buyback is part of its capital policy balancing stable dividend returns (targeting a payout ratio of approximately 40% over the medium to long term) with opportunistic repurchases based on share price levels and market conditions. The company also disclosed that two major shareholders—Aioi Nissay Dowa Insurance (19.6 million shares, 0.98% ownership as of end-March 2024) and Sompo Japan Insurance (24.1 million shares, 1.21% ownership as of end-June 2024)—have separately indicated plans to reduce policy holdings by end-March 2029 and end-March 2031 respectively, specifying no preference as to selling method or counterparty.

The interim dividend for FY3/26 is maintained at ¥53 per share, resulting in a full-year dividend of ¥106 per share (unchanged from the prior year).
